  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Sawcut joints were executed too late, resulting in random shrinkage cracking (FD) outside the intended joint lines."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Evaluate the depth and extent of the random cracks.",
    "Determine if structural integrity is compromised or if cosmetic/epoxy injection repair is acceptable to the owner.",
    "Review joint-cutting schedule and timing protocols for future pours."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to ACI 302 and 308, contraction joints must be sawed as soon as the concrete is hard enough to resist raveling but before random cracking occurs (typically within 4 to 12 hours of finishing). Cutting late defeats the purpose of the joints, leading to uncontrolled cracking.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 302.1R (Guide for Concrete Floor and Slab Construction)",
    "ACI 308R (Guide to External Curing of Concrete)"
  ]
}
